ety: act. part. of اوْرث 'to make (one
def: part. adj. & s.m. Making (one) heir (to, -kā); entailing; causing; — a legator; the person from whom an inheritance is derived (whether in the descending or ascending line); — a cause; author.
more: n. m. The legator; the person from whom an inheritance is derived, whether in the descending or ascending line. mu'ris-i-ālā , The head or common legator.
(part. act. IV. of ورث Heb. ) s. m. A cause, an author.

ety: munḍ, q.v.+Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. A bald-head, a shaveling, a contemptuous term for a gosāṅʼīṅ, or a bairāgī (because they shave their heads wholly); — the Devanāgarī character written without the upper line (see kaithī).

ety: S. मूर्छनं; or fr. murjhānā
def: s.m. Withering, fading, drooping, pining; fainting, swooning, syncope.

ety: murjhā˚ = S. मूर्छ(ति), rt. मुर्छ्+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि, caus. augment; — or = S. मूर्छित
def: v.n. To wither, fade, droop, pine; to become dejected or dispirited; to turn faint; to swoon
more: v. n. S. मुर्च्छन 1. To droop; wither. Jo bhādoṅ māṅ khāṅg khakhāve, harā khet chhan māṅ murjhāve . Rus. In August if the wind blows high too soon the green fields withered lie. 2. To pine; fret. Āvat haiṅ to haṅs mile, aur chalat rahe murjhāë, Tulsī! aise mintr ke koṭ phāṅd-ke jāë . Tulsī Dās. Who meets you with glad laughter and in sorrow parts from you, Tulsī! high ramparts one might scale to greet a friend so true.
v. n. To wither, to fade, to pine, to droop, to be dejected or dispirited.
subs: v.n. (intens.), To become blighted or withered; to wither or fade away; to faint, to swoon ( = murćhā-jānā).

ety: act. part. of ارجأ 'to postpone, put off,' &c., iv of رجأ, not in use
def: s.m. One who delays doing what he promises; a procrastinator; — one of the sect called murjiʼat (or murjiyat), a sect of Muslim antinomians (who assert that faith consists in words without works, and that if they do not pray nor fast, their faith will save them); — name of a tribe.
more: (part. act. IV. of رجأ) 1. adj. One who delays doing what he promises. 2. One of the sect called مرجيت who procrastinate, or think good works unnecessary and faith sufficient. 3. s. m. Name of a tribe.

ety: caus. of muraknā
def: v.t. To cause to twist or curve; to twist, curve, curl, bend; to cause to turn away; — v.n. To twist; to writhe, &c. ( = muraknā, q.v.).
more: v. a. See muraknā , q. v.
v. n. To twist, to writhe.

def: adj. & s.m. Stupid, foolish, silly, idiotic; ignorant, unwise, inconsiderate; — a stupid fellow, ignoramus, fool, blockhead, idiot
subs: s.f. The insisting of an ignorant fellow upon what he says, ignorant obstinacy; stubbornness
s.m. pl. Ignoramuses, dolts, blockheads, clowns, fools, idiots.

more: n. f. Earrings worn by men.
s. f. 1. An ornament worn in the ear. 2. The tragus of the ear.

def: s.f. & adv. = مرکونڐي murkūnḍī, q.v. s.f. A posture (in sitting, or lying); sitting with the head upon the knees and the arms folded around them; or (as by some ascetics) with the head depressed and the legs brought up over the neck; — adv. With the head upon the knees, &c.; head-downwards
subs: To sit in the posture described above; to lie doubled up in bed; to take to bed, to be or become bedrid.

r: murkūṅḍī, mur, muṛ, muṅḍ, kunḍī, kunḍlī
ety: mur = muṛ = muṅḍ, q.v.+kunḍī = kunḍlī, q.v.
def: s.f. A posture (in sitting, or lying); sitting with the head upon the knees and the arms folded around them; or (as by some ascetics) with the head depressed and the legs brought up over the neck; — adv. With the head upon the knees, &c.; head-downwards
more: Head downwards, head reversed(?). مرکونڐي مارنا murkūṅḍī mārnā, v. (dakh.) To take to the bed, to be confined to bed or to be bedrid.
subs: To sit in the posture described above; to lie doubled up in bed; to take to bed, to be or become bedrid.

ety: prob. S. मुण्ड+ल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Toothless.
more: adj. 1. Toothless. 2. (Sans. मयूर ) s. m. A peacock.

ety: i.q. muṇḍlā, q.v.
def: adj. (f. -ī), Shaved.
s.f. = مرمر murmur, q.v.

ety: murmur+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Crackling; crisp, &c.; — rice pressed flat (used as food); swollen, parched rice; — a kind of fringe made of gold or silver
more: Bhoj. n. m. Swollen, parched rice. murmuroṅ kā thailā , n. m. lit . a bag full of swollen parched rice. A fat, corpulent person; an unwieldy mass of flesh.
s. m. A kind of food, rice pressed flat and eaten raw. (So named in imitation of the sound which it gives in chewing).
subs: s.m. 'A bag full of parched rice'; a corpulent, lubberly person; an unwieldy mass of flesh

def: v. n. To crackle; crunch; scrunch. marmarānā, murmurānā , v. n. To murmur; grumble ( buṛbuṛānā ). murmurī haḍḍī , Cartilage; gristle.
v.n. (rustic or dialec.) = muṅḍnā, q.v. v.n. To be shut, or closed; to be filled up, be blocked up; to be contracted; — v.t. To close, &c. ( = mūṅdnā, q.v.)

ety: see the trans. moṛnā
def: v.n. To be turned; to be turned back, or down, or over; to be or become twisted; to be or become bent, or crooked; — to turn; to bend; to twine; to double over, or under, or back; to pucker up (as cloth, paper, leaves, &c.); — to turn back, to return; — to turn or draw aside; to diverge
more: v. n. To be turned back, bent, twisted, turned, to turn, bend, twine.
subs: v.n. (intens.) To be or become turned, or bent, or twisted, &c. (see muṛnā).

ety: i.q. muṛnā, q.v.
def: v.n. (dialec.) To be saturated or drenched (with); to be absorbed or wrapt (in); to be swallowed up and lost (in); to be bent (on); to be addicted (to); to acquire a taste (for).
more: v. n. 1. To be bent, twisted. 2. To be turned back. 3. See phirnā , 2, 3, 7. muṛvā'nā . Caus. of moṛnā , q. v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀭𑀇 (maraï), from Sanskrit मर॑ति (márati).

def: s.m. Myrrh.

ety: mur = muṛ(nā), q.v.+Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. A squib.

ety: pass. part. of ارسل 'to send' (a message, or a messenger
def: part. adj. & s.m. Sent (to another), despatched (esp. with a message); — one sent with a message; a messenger; an ambassador; an apostle; a prophet; the prophet Moḥammad; — mursil (act. part. of the same verb), s.m. Sender (of a message, or an ambassador, &c.), despatcher
more: part. pass. (IV. of رسل) Sent to another, especially with a message; s. m. A messenger, a divine messenger, the prophet, an apostle. (Of this first class the Muhammadans reckon 313: the whole number of prophets (انبيا) are reckoned by the most moderate to amount to 124,000).
subs: s.m. The prophet sent, i.e. Moḥammad.

ety: for A. مرسلة mursalat, fem. of mursal
def: part. = mursal, q.v.; — s.m. A letter, epistle, missive (pl. mursalāt)
more: n. m. 1. A letter sent; a despatch. 2. A sender; despatcher. mursilah , adj. Sent; despatched.
s. m. 1. An epistle. 2. A necklace.
subs: s.m. A clasp for a necklace.

def: (obl. pl. of mursal), s.m. pl. Apostles; prophets; — saiyiduʼl-mursalīn, Prince of the apostles, i.e. Moḥammad.

ety: act. part. of ارشد 'to cause (one to take or follow a right way or course,' iv of رشد 'to take or follow a right way,' &c.
def: s.m. A guide to the right way (i.e. to salvation), a spiritual director or guide; director, instructor, monitor; — the head of a religious order; — (ironically) a knowing fellow; a knave.
more: n. m. رشد took the right way. 1. A spiritual director or guide; a religious instructor. 2. A head of a religious order. 3. ( Slang ) A knowing fellow; a knave.
(part. act. IV. of رشد) s. m. An instructor, a director, guide (spiritual), a teacher, a monitor.

ety: fr. murshid
def: s.f. Guidance, instruction in matters of religion.
more: s. f. Instruction in matters of religion, guidance.

ety: for A. مرسلة mursilat, fem. of mursil
def: s.m. A sender, despatcher ( = mursil, q.v.).

ety: act. part. of ارتدّ 'to return'; 'to apostatize,' &c., viii of ردّ 'to cause (one
def: s.m. An apostate (esp. fr. Muḥammadanism to disbelief), a renegade
more: Mah. n. m. رد casting off. An apostate; infidel; a renegade. murtad h. , v. n. To apostatize.
(part. pass. VIII. of ردّ Heb. ) s. m. An apostate, renegado. مرتد هونا murtadd honā, v. n. To apostatize.
subs: v.n. To become an apostate, to apostatize.

ety: act. or pass. part. of ارتفع 'to become raised, or high, or elevated,' &c., viii of رفع 'to upraise, to elevate,' &c.
def: part. adj. Elevated, exalted, high, lofty, sublime, eminent; aggrandized.
more: adj. رفع raised. Elevated; exalted; high; sublime; eminent.
(part. pass. or act. VIII. of رفع) adj. Elevated, exalted, high, sublime, eminent, aggrandized.

def: part. Pledged, pawned; mortgaged
more: (part. pass. VIII. of رهن) adj. Pledged, pawned.
subs: (act. part. of the same verb), s.m. One who takes or receives a pledge; a pawnbroker; a mortgagee
or murtahin-ě-qābiẓ, s.m. A mortgagee in possession.

def: adj. رﮩن fixed, put, deposited. Pledged; pawned. murtah'in , n. m. A mortgagee. murtahin dakhīl, yā qābiz . A mortgagee in possession.

ety: act. part. of ارتعش, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Trembling, quivering, quaking; dreading.
more: part. act. (VIII. of رعش Heb. ) Trembling.

ety: act. part. of ارتکب, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Mounted, riding; carried, borne; embarking (in), venturing (upon), engaging (in), undertaking, attempting, encountering; committing, perpetrating (a crime); implicated (in), guilty (of); — aggressor, perpetrator
more: adj. رکب rode. 1. Committing; perpetrating. 2. Guilty. murtakib-i-jurm . One who commits a crime.

(part. act. VIII. of رکب Heb. ) adj. 1. Mounted, carried, riding. 2. Perpetrating, committing (a crime); aggressor, perpetrator. مرتکب هونا murtakib honā, To perpetrate, to commit (a crime), to be an aggressor.
subs: (-meṅ), To embark (in), venture (on), &c.; to be an aggressor.

ety: pass. part. of ارتسم 'to be marked, or stamped,' &c., viii of رسم 'to mark, or stamp'; see rasm
def: part. Marked, stamped, depicted (in), drawn, traced, sketched, &c.

ety: for A. مرتش murtashiṉ, act. part. of ارتشي 'to take or receive a bribe' (rishwat
def: part. adj. & s.m. Addicted to taking or receiving bribes; corrupt; — a taker of bribes.

ety: pass. part. of ارتاض 'to be broken, or trained,' viii of روض 'to break, or train'
def: part. Broken in; trained; disciplined; exercised; instructed.
more: part. act. (VIII. from روض Heb. ) Exercised, disciplined, instructed.

ety: pass. part. of ارتضی, viii of (and syn. with
def: part. Chosen, approved; agreeable; — one of the titles of 'Alī.
more: (part. pass. VIII. of رضو Heb. ) adj. 1. Chosen, approved. 2. One of the titles of Aﻌlī.

ety: act. part. of ارطب 'to make moist,' &c., iv of رطب
def: part. adj. Humid, moist, wet; juicy, succulent; verdant; fresh, ripe (syn. rut̤ab).
more: (part. act. IV. of رطب Heb. ) adj. 1. Humid, moist. 2. Verdant. ¶ murat̤t̤ib (part. act. II.) Making moist.

r: mūrtti, mūrti, mūrtī, mūrt, mūrat, mūrati
def: s.f. A solid body; a tangible or material form, anything which has definite shape and limits; the human frame; body; form; visible shape; matter, substance; — embodiment; incarnation; personification; — an image, an idol; a statue, a picture; flgure, form (the most com. meanings); — a holy person, a faqīr; — a person, a man, an individual (com. used by Bairāgīs)
more: n. f. S. मूर्ति form, Pr. muttī . A statue; idol ( but , 1). 2. A picture; portrait. Is mandar bich nirakh tū! kyā raṅg baraṅgī mūrat hai! Hirde se tanak parakh tū, is mūrat meṅ kyā sūrat hai! Hymn. Mauj. What wondrous art is here! behold this temple fair! What various forms and hues are in this image rare! 3. A holy man; a faqīr . 4. An individual. Bābā! do mūrtiyāṅ yāṅ bhī baiṭhī haiṅ. mūrtī asthāpan, pirtishṭhā , n. m. Installation of an idol. mūrtī-pūjan ; P. but-parastī , n. f. Idolatry. mūrtī-khanḍan , Image-breaking ( butshikanī ).
s. f. 1. A statue, image, an idol, a picture, a resemblance, form, figure, body. 2. A person (term used by Bairāgīs ).
subs: vulg. mūrtī-asthāpan, s.m., and mūrti-pratishṭhā, vulg. mūrtī-pirtishṭhā, s.f. Installation of an idol

or mūrti-mant (f. -matī), or mūrati-vant (f. -vatī), adj. & s.m. Having a material or bodily form; endowed with a corporeal frame; material; embodied; corporeal; incarnate; personified; — accomplished, clever.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit मूर्ति (mūrti, “embodiment, form”).

ety: inf. n. of مرّ 'to pass by, or through,' &c.
def: s.m. Passing by or over, elapsing; passage, pass, avenue; transition; transit; — adj. Passing, present, modern
more: (inf. of مرّ) s. m. Passing, elapsing, a passage, pass, transition, avenue.
subs: adv. In lapse of time; lapsed; ago.

ety: for A. مروة, inf. n. of مرؤ 'to be bold or manly,' &c.
def: s.f. Manhood, manliness, virility, fortitude; — human nature; humanity; — generosity; benevolence; kindness; — urbanity, affability, politeness
more: n. f. Urbanity; politeness. 2. Benevolence; kindness; generosity. muravvat toṛnā , v. a. To withdraw one's regard from. muravvat k. , v. a. To show kindness or regard. muravvat-vālā . 1. Gracious; affable. 2. Kind. be-muravvat , adj. Unkind; ungracious; unfriendly.
n. m. 1. (H. mārnā to kill) 1. Rent-free land given to the families of retainers killed in battle. 2. Painting the face of a bridegroom with rolī , q. v.
(from مرأ Heb. and Chald. ) s. f. 1. Manliness, virility, fortitude. 2. Affection, humanity, generosity, urbanity, affability. مروت توڙنا murūwat toṛnā, To cease behaving affectionately.
subs: (apnī), To cease treating with kindness or affection; to put an end to kindly feeling (between); to withdraw (one's) regard (from)
(-se), To practise kindness or civility (towards), show kindness or regard (to), deal kindly, &c. (with)
adj. Inhuman; unkind; ungracious; uncivil, rude, unpolished.

def: s.m. Appointer, preparer, causer; the First cause
more: n. m. ( Log .) An effect (S. kārya ) musabbab-ul-asbāb , n. m. The first cause. musabbab-i-haqīqī , n. m. The real cause; God.
(part. act. II. of سبب) s. m. The causer, the first cause. مسبب الاسباب musabbibu-l-asbāb, The causer of causes, i. e. God. مسبب حقيقي musabbib-i-ḥaḳīḳī, God.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Appointed, prepared, caused; — s.m. That which is appointed, or caused; an effect
s.m. The Appointer, or Preparer, of means or causes, the Causer of causes, an epithet applied to God
s.m. The true cause, i.e. God.

def: s.m. corr. of مسور musawwir, q.v. part. adj. & s.m. Forming, fashioning, &c.; — former, fashioner; painter, limner, drawer; sculptor
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Formed, fashioned; painted, &c.

ety: pass. part. of اسعد 'to render prosperous, or fortunate, or happy,' iv of سعد 'to be or become prosperous, &c.'
def: adj. Fortunate, happy, &c. (see next, which is the more common form.
more: (part. pass. IV. from سعد Heb. ) adj. Happy, fortunate, propitious, august.

def: W. The wooden scrapers for scraping out the boiled juice of the sugarcane.

ety: for A. مصدقة muṣaddaqat, fem. of muṣaddaq
def: part. = muṣaddaq, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of سدّس 'to make six of, or to make six-cornered,' &c., ii of سدس 'to be or become the sixth of'
def: part. adj. Composed of six; six-sided; hexagonal; cubic; — s.m. Verse consisting of six lines, hexameter; — a six-sided figure, a hexagon; a cube.
more: n. m. سدس six. 1. A hexagon. 2. A hexameter.
(part. pass. II. of سدس) 1. adj. Composed of six; sexangular. 2. s. m. A kind of stanza consisting of six lines, sextain: hexameter. 3. A hexagon.

ety: act. part. of صدّع 'to affect with headache,' ii of صدع 'to cleave, split'
def: adj. & s.m. 'Making the head ache'; troublesome; — a troubler.
more: (part. act. II. of صدع) adj. Troublesome, troubler.

ety: act. part. of صدق 'to prove to be truthful, or true; to confirm the truth of,' ii of صدق 'to prove (or turn out
def: part. adj. & s.m. Proving to be true, verifying; — a verifier; — an affirmer; — one who believes another
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Proved to be true, verified, confirmed; attested; authenticated.

ety: act. part. of صادم 'to dash or strike (against
def: adj. Striking; mutually striking; colliding (with).
more: (part. act. iii. of صدم) adj. Striking, mutually striking.

ety: for A. مصافحة muṣāfaḥat, inf. n. of صافح 'to take by the hand; to join hands,' iii of صفح 'to turn (towards
def: s.m. Joining hands, shaking hands.
more: Mah. n. m. Shaking hands. musāfaha k. , Mah. v. a. To shake hands.
(part. act. III. of صفح) s. m. Shaking hands.

ety: for A. مسافحة, inf. n. of سافح 'to commit fornication with,' iii of سفح 'to pour out or forth'
def: s.f. Fornication, adultery.
more: (inf. III. of سفح) s. f. Adultery.

ety: for A. مسافرة, inf. n. of سافر; see musāfir
def: s.f. Travelling, journeying; dying; — travel, journey; — a foreign country.
more: n. f. 1. Travelling (سفر). 2. A foreign country. musāfrī kā asbāb , Camp furniture.
(inf. III. of سفر) s. f. Travelling, travel, journey.

ety: for A. مصفي muṣaffā, pass. part. of صفّي 'to clear, or clarify, or purify,' ii of صفا (for صفو
def: adj. Clarified, clear, clean, pure
more: adj. صفو Clarified; pure. musaffī , adj. Purifying; refining. musaffī-i-khūn , Blood-purifying.
(prop. مصفيٰٰ part. pass. II. of صفو) adj. Clean, clarified, pure.
subs: (act. part. of the same verb), part. adj. & s.m. Clearing, clarifying; purifying; refining; — clearer, purifier, &c.
Clearer or purifier of the blood.

ety: act. part. of سافر 'to journey,' iii of سفر 'to go forth to journey'
def: s.m. A traveller, wayfarer; passenger; temporary sojourner; stranger
more: n. m. سفر journey. A traveller (بنَاؤ). musāfir-parvarī, musāfir-navāzī , n. f. Protection or comfort of travellers. musāfir-khāna , n. m. An inn; a caravanserai. musāfirā'na , adv. As a traveller. musāfirāna guzrān . Roughing it as a traveller.
(part. act. III. of سفر) s. m. A traveller, passenger, stranger. مسافرپروري musāfir-parwar-ī, or مسافرنوازي musāfir-nawāz-ī, s. f. Protection of travellers, hospitality.
subs: or musāfir-nawāzī, s.f. Protection or comfort of travellers; hospitality
s.m. 'House for travellers,' an inn, a caravansary.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُسَافِر (musāfir), borrowed from Arabic مُسَافِر (musāfir). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi مسافر (msafr /⁠musāfir⁠/).

ety: musāfir, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: adv. Traveller-like; as a traveller
subs: Faring, or roughing it, as a traveller.

ety: fr. musāfir
def: s.f. = musāfarat, q.v.
subs: s.m. Travelling equipage or appurtenances; camp-furniture.

ety: for A. مصاحبة, inf. n. of صاحب; see muṣāḥib
def: s.f. Companionship, company, society, familiarity, communion.
more: (inf. III. of صحب) s. f. Companionship, society, communion.

ety: for A. مساهلة musāhalat, inf. n. of ساهل 'to be easy, or facile with; to be gentle with,' iii of سہل 'to be or become easy,' &c.
def: s.m. Negligence, carelessness.
more: (inf. III. of سہل) s. m. Negligence, carelessness.

def: s.m. (dialec.) A wild man, a man of the woods.

ety: act. part. of صحّح 'to render sound, or correct,' &c., ii of صحّ 'to be sound, or correct,' &c.
def: s.m. One who revises, or corrects; a mender; an emendator; a verifier.
more: n. m. One who revises or corrects.

ety: act. part. of صاحب 'to associate, or consort (with
def: s.m. A companion, an associate; a friend; favourite (of a prince); an aide-de-camp (pl. muṣāḥibān).
more: n. m. 1. A companion; an associate. 2. An aide-de-camp.
(part. act. III. of صحب) s. m. f. A companion, friend, favourite, aide-de-camp, associate. (Pers. plur. مصاحبان).

ety: fr. muṣāḥib
def: s.f. Companionship, &c. (i.q. muṣāḥabat).
(part. act. f. III. of سحق Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Mulier quæ aliæ mulieri se pro viro gerit, quæ confrictu libidinem alterius explet.

ety: for A. مساحقة musāḥiqat, part. act. fem. of ساحق, iii of سحق 'to rub,' &c.
def: s.m. Mulier quae aliae mulieri se pro viro gerit, quae confrictu libidinem alterius explet.
s. f. Judaism (following of Moses).

ety: fr. mūsā, 'Moses'
def: s.f. The religion of Moses, Judaism.

ety: act. part. of ساعد; see next
def: part. adj. Aiding, assisting, helping; favourable.
more: (part. act. III. of سعد) adj. Assisting, aiding, favourable.

ety: pass. part. of سجّع, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Rhyming, rhythmical (speech or language); harmonious, or measured (periods); — rhythmical prose.
more: adj. سجع measured, uniform. ( Rhet .) Rhythmical (periods).
(part. pass. II. of سجع) Rhythmical (speech), harmonious or measured (periods).

ety: pass. part. of سجّل 'to decide judicially, and record the sentence,' ii of سجل 'to pour out or forth continually'
def: part. adj. Judicially decided and recorded; proved, authenticated, and sealed (by a Qāẓī or judge).
more: (part. pass. II. of سجل) adj. Proved, authenticated by a judge.

ety: pass. part. of سخّر 'to constrain, or compel; to bring under subjection,' &c., ii of سخر, idem
def: part. adj. Brought under subjection, subjected, subdued, overcome, conquered, taken.
more: (part. pass. II. of سخر) adj. Subjected, subdued, overcome.

ety: derived by Shakspear fr. A. مسك 'to restrain'; but prob. fr. मुसक (for मुस्क), a dialec. form of मुख+S. क+कः
def: s.m. (dialec.) An ox-muzzle.
more: (from مسک Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A muzzle, used to prevent oxen from eating grain.

ety: pass. part. of سقّف, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Having a ceiling or roof, roofed; floored.

ety: act. part. of سکّن 'to cause to be still or calm, &c.; to allay, assuage,' &c. ii of سکن 'to be or become still,' &c.
def: s.m. Allayer, quieter, &c.; an anodyne (pl. musakkināt).
s.m. A long, heavy, wooden pestle used in clearing rice from the husk (vulg. mūslā, q.v.); a pounder; — a club, mace; — name of the twenty-second yoga or division of the moon's path
more: s. m. A long, heavy, wooden pestle, used in clearing rice from the husk. مسل دهاربرسنا musal dhār barasnā, v. n. To rain very heavily.
subs: s.m. 'The bearer of the club,' an epithet of Baladev; — ʻclub-stream,' very heavy rain

ety: Prk. मूसलं; S. मुसलं
def: s.m. A pestle; a long heavy wooden pestle used for husking rice, &c.; — a club
more: n. m. Dim. mūslī , n. f. S. मुसल, , Pālī, musalo . 1. A long heavy wooden pestle with which grain is beaten in a mortar. 1. Apnā ghar sanjhaut na, ankā ghar mūsar aisan bātī . Prov. Bhoj. In her own house not even a farthing rush-light, in another's house a wick she'll burn as a pestle thick. 2. Chhatrī bhagat, na mūsal dhanvī . Nor Chhatrī anchorite, nor bow of rammer made. (Can the Ethiopian change his skin?). 3. Hāl meṅ phāl, dahī meṅ mūsal . Prov. A share in the plough, a pestle in tyre. (Said to an impertinent intruder). 2. The penis. Hamāre mūsar se. mūslā-dhār , n. m. Heavy rain. mūslā-dhār barasnā , v. n. To rain in torrents; to rain cats and dogs. mūsloṅ ḍhol bajānā , v. a. To proclaim by beat of drum. mūs'lā , n. m. A rod; piston; bolt.
s. m. A wooden pestle. موسلان سون ڐهول بجانا mūslāṅ sūṅ ḍhol bajānā (dakh.) To feast, to make merry.
subs: or mūsal-dhārā, s.m. = mūslā-dhār, and musal-dhār, qq.v.

r: muṣālaḥa, muṣālaḥat, muṣālaḥa karnā, se, meṅ
def: s.m. = P مصالحت muṣālaḥat, s.f. (for A. مصالحة muṣālaḥat, inf. n. of صالح 'to make peace or reconciliation with,' iii of صلح 'to be or become good,' &c.), Pacification, reconciliation, composition, compromise
more: (inf. III. of صلح Heb. &c. ) s. m. Reconciliation, pacification, composition, compromise. مصالحه کرنا muṣālaḥa karnā, To adjust, compound, compromise.
subs: (-se, or -meṅ), To make peace (with, or between); to become reconciled (with); to reconcile; to adjust, compound, compromise.
wety: From Classical Persian مصالح (maṣāliḥ, “affairs, materials, spices”), a plural of مصلحت (maslahat), itself borrowing from Arabic مَصْلَحَة (maṣlaḥa).

r: muṣālaḥat, muṣālaḥa, muṣālaḥa karnā, se, meṅ
def: s.f. = P مصالحه muṣālaḥa, s.m. (for A. مصالحة muṣālaḥat, inf. n. of صالح 'to make peace or reconciliation with,' iii of صلح 'to be or become good,' &c.), Pacification, reconciliation, composition, compromise
subs: (-se, or -meṅ), To make peace (with, or between); to become reconciled (with); to reconcile; to adjust, compound, compromise.

def: adj. & s.m. = musalin, q.v.; — s.f. The plant Curculigo orchioides; — the house-lizard (also written mushalī).
adj. & s.m. = musalin, q.v. — s.f. = musalī, and mūsalī, qq.v. adj. & s.m. Armed with a club; — an epithet of Śiva, of Balarām, and of Baladev.

ety: fr. musal
def: adj. & s.m. Armed with a club; — an epithet of Śiva, of Balarām, and of Baladev.
s.m. = مصلا muṣallā, q.v. s.m. A place of prayer (syn. ʻīd-gāh); an oratory; a mosque; — a carpet, or mat, for saying the appointed prayers upon.

ety: for A. مصلَي muṣallā, n. of place fr. صلّي 'to pray,' ii of صلو; see ṣalāt
def: s.m. A place of prayer (syn. ʻīd-gāh); an oratory; a mosque; — a carpet, or mat, for saying the appointed prayers upon.
more: n. m. صلي 1. A carpet to pray on. 1. Bas ho chukī namāz, musallā uṭhāīye! Prov. Enough, the prayers are over, take up the carpet. 2. Masalla pasār, ba/?/gal meṅ yār . Prov. The carpet spread for prayer, Phyllis embracing e'er. 2. A place of prayer.
(part. pass. II. from صلي Chald. ) s. m. A carpet to pray on, a place of prayer.

ety: pass. part. of سلّح 'to arm (a person
def: part. adj. Armed with a weapon or weapons, equipped with arms; clad in mail
more: adj. سلح Armed. musallah-jaṅg , adj. Armed for fight. musallah h. , v. n. To arm.
(part. pass. II. of سلح Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Armed for war, in armour.
subs: or musallaḥ-jang, adj. Armed for war
v.t. To equip with arms, to arm; to dress or clothe in mail
v.n. To be or become armed; to arm oneself; to be clad in mail.

ety: pass. part. of سلّم 'to cause to be safe, secure, or free,' &c., ii of سلم 'to be or become safe,' &c.
def: part. adj. Preserved, kept; safe, secure; free, exempted (from); sound; complete, entire, whole; — committed (to), consigned, intrusted, given in charge; — accepted, admitted, granted; — adv. Exclusively, solely, &c.
more: adj. سلم was safe. 1. Entire; whole. 2. Sound. 3. Accepted; admitted.
subs: (act. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Keeping safe, preserving in liberty, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُسْلِم (muslim), borrowed from Arabic مُسْلِم (muslim), from أَسْلَمَ (ʔaslama).

ety: pass. part. of ثلّث 'to make (it
def: part. adj. & s.m. Having three angles or corners; three-cornered; triangular; trilateral; — a triangle (syn. tikhūṅṭ); — a sort of verse consisting of three lines
more: adj. ثلث taking a third, becoming a third. 1. ( Geom .) A triangle ( tikhūṅṭ ). 2. A kind of verse of three lines. musallas hād-ul-zāviyā ; S. मसकोण त्रिभुज; H. chhuṭ-koniyā tikhūṅṭ , ( Geom .) An acute-angled triangle. musallas qāëmah-ul-zāviyā ; S. समत्रिबाहु त्रिभुज; H. pur-koniyā tikhūṅt . A right-angled triangle. musallas mutasāvī-ul-azlā ; S. समद्विबाहु त्रिभुज; H. barābar-bhuj tikhūṅṭ . An equilateral triangle. musallas mutasāvī-ul-sāqain ; S. विषम त्रिभुज; H. do bhuj barābar tikhūṅṭ . An isosceles triangle. musallas mukhtalif-ul-azlā ; S. अधिककोण H. nā-barābar-bhuj tikhūṅṭ . A scalene triangle. musallas munfarajah-ul-zāviyā ; S. त्रिभुज; H. baṛh-koniyāṅ tikhūṅṭ . An obtuse-angled triangle. ilm-i-musallas; ṭikhūṅṭ-nāp ; S. त्रिकोणमिति Trignometry. musal'lasī ; H. ti-konyā , adj. Triangular. musallasī hissā , n. m. ( Arch .) The spandrel. musallasī shīshah . A prism.
(part. pass. II. of ثلث Heb. ) adj. Three-cornered, triple. s. m. A triangle, a sort of verse consisting of three lines. مثلث مثلا mus̤allas̤ mas̤lā, An allegorical fable.

ety: rel. n. fr. mus̤allas̤
def: adj. Triangular; trilateral

ety: pass. part. of سلط 'to cause to overcome or prevail, or have power or dominion' (over
def: part. adj. & s.m. Conquered, subdued; appointed, set (over); — one who is appointed or set (over), a governor, ruler; — conquest
more: (part. pass. II. of سلط Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. 1. A governour, a ruler, conqueror. 2. Conquest.
subs: (act part. of the same verb), part. adj. & s.m. Overcoming, subduing; predominant; superior; — a conqueror; a governor, ruler.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُسَلَّط (musallat), borrowed from Arabic مُسَلَّط (musallaṭ), from سَلَّطَ (sallaṭa).

ety: for A. مصلٍّ muṣalliṉ, act. part. of صلّی 'to pray'; see ṣalāt
def: adj. & s.m. Praying; — holy, righteous; — one who prays.
more: (part. act. II. of صلي) adj. Praying, one who prays; holy, righteous. ¶ musallạ̄, s. f. See مصلا muṣallā.

ety: prob. corr. fr. A. pl. muslimīn; but said by some to be the Pers. pl. muslimān; and by others to be = muslim-mān 'like a muslim'
def: s.m. A believer in the religion of Moḥammad, a Moḥammadan, a Musalmān (i.q. Muslim, q.v.)
more: n. m. A believer in Mahammad; a Mahomedan. Dīyā dān māṅge Musalmān . (Said by Hindūs). Prov. The jointure with the daughter given, the daughter dead the Musalman takes back. musalmān k. , v. a. To make a Mahomedan of. musalmān h. , v. n. To be converted to Mahomedanism.
(from سلم) s. m. A believer, a Muhammadan. (Pers. plur. مسلمانان).
subs: v.t. To make a Moḥammadan of
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُسَلْمَان (musalmān), from Arabic مُسْلِم (muslim). First attested as Middle Hindi مسلمان (musalamān), Old Hindi मुसलमांन (musalamāṃna).

def: s.f. The Moḥammadan religion, Moḥammadanism; — (in Hind.) adj. Of or relating to the religion of Moḥammad, Moḥammadan; — s.f. A Moḥammadan or Musalmān woman; — circumcision
more: adj. Of or relating to Mahomedanism. Musalmāṅ dar gor, va musalmānī dar kitāb . Prov. The Musalmen are in their graves, their faith dwells in the Book. (No true Mahomedans are left). musalmān'ī , n. f. 1. A Mahomedan woman. 2. Circumcision. musalmānī k. , v. a. To circumcise.
s. f. 1. The muhammadan religion. 2. (vulg.) Circumcision. 3. (Hind.) A female musalmān. 4. adj. Of or relating to a musalmān.

ety: pass. part. of the quadrilit. verb سُلْسل 'to be connected; to be bound with a silsila or "chain"'
def: part. adj. Bound with the silsila, q.v.; chained, enchained, linked, concatenated; sewed, connected, or fastened together; coherent; successive, consecutive; placed in order; — s.f. A kind of lace or fringe.
more: adj. سل drew out. Concatenated; successive ( silsilevār ). musalsal , n. f. A kind of lace or fringe.
(part. pass. I. of quadrilit. سلسل) adj. Concatenated, successive, linked, chained. s. A kind of lace or fringe.

ety: for A. مسامحة musāmaḥat, inf. n. of سامح, iii of (and syn. with
def: s.m. Treating (anyone) gently, acting with lenity; pardoning; — lenity; connivance; negligence; — languor (pl. musāmaḥāt).
more: (inf. III. of سمح Heb. ) s. m. 1. Treating any one gently, pardoning. 2. Negligence.

def: part. adj. See مسما musammā.
more: part. pass. (II. of سمو Heb. and Aram. ) Named, denominated, entitled, called.

ety: for A. مسمَي musammā, pass. part. of سمّي 'to name, to call (one
def: part. adj. Named, called, styled; entitled, denominated; — s.m. Thing named.

(part. pass. II. of صمّ Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Fixed, concluded, determined.
subs: v.n. To be fixed or determined; to be made up (the mind).

ety: pass. part. of ثمّن 'to make eight,' ii of ثمن 'to be or become the eighth (of

(part. pass. II. of ثمن Heb. ) adj. Consisting of eight, octangular, eightfold; octagon.

ety: pass. part. of سمّن 'to render fat, or plump,' ii of سمن 'to be or become fat or plump'
def: part. Fattened, made fat.

ety: pass. part. of ثمّر, ii of ثمر 'to bear fruit'
def: part. adj. Ripe for gathering; fruitful; — s.m. Fruit ripe for gathering.
n. f. A title prefixed to the names of women. musammāt , adj. Called (a lady).

ety: for A. مسماة, fem. of musammā
def: adj. = musammā, q.v.; — s.f. A title prefixed to the names of women; lady.
s.m. (dialec.) A heap of dust (prob. a var. form of masān).

ety: S. मूषणं
def: s.m. Stealing, pilfering, filching.

ety: caus. of mūsnā
def: v.t. To cause to steal.
more: (causal of موسنا मूषण r. मूष Steal) v. a. To cause to steal.

def: s.f. (dialec.) A violent rush (as into a crowd) with the body inclined forwards and the head depressed; charge, onset, attack, sally; — effort, attempt.
more: s. f. (dakh.) 1. Design, purpose, determination: see قصد ḳaṣd. 2. Assault, attack: see حمله ḥamla.

ety: pass. part. of ثني 'to make two, to dualize,' &c., ii of ثني 'to double,' &c.
def: part. adj. Doubled, double, two-fold, binary; dualized; dual, of the dual number; marked with two points, double-dotted (a letter)
more: n. m. A duplicate; counterpart; counterfoil. musannā , adj. Double; binary; duplicate. musannā-beh , n. The original (of a copy).
(part. pass. II. of ثني Heb. ) 1. s. m. A copy, duplicate, the second. 2. adj. Double, binary, dual, of the dual number.
subs: a copy; the second; a counterpart; a junior or younger (as distinguished from the father of the same name); — (in Gram.) the dual number
vulg. mus̤annā-běh, s.m. 'That of which it is the copy,' the original of a copy.

ety: pass. part. of صنّع, ii of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Wrought; factitious; artificial (i.q. maṣnūʻ).
more: (part. pass. II. of صنع) adj. Factitious, artificial.

def: s.m.(?) pl. (of muṣannaf), Compositions, writings, works, books.

ety: act. part. of صنّف 'to assort; to compose' (a book

v.t. To obtain or take as a loan or trust; to borrow.

ety: act. part. of استبدّ 'to be or become alone, or independent of others,' x of بدّ 'to make to retire,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Acting (or one who acts) alone; possessed of (or a claimant of) exclusive right or property.
more: (part. act. X. of بدّ) adj. Alone, acting alone.

ety: pass. part. of استدام, x of (and syn. with
def: adj. Continual; perpetual; constant, ancient, immemorial; — assiduous.
more: (part. pass. X. of دوم) adj. Perpetual.

ety: act. part. of استدعي, x of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. & s.m. Calling for, requesting, demanding; — one who requests, or solicits, or petitions; requester; wisher, desirer; petitioner; applicant; suitor; claimant.

ety: act. part. of استدار 'to have (or to assume
def: adj. Having a round or circular form; round, circular; spherical, globular
more: adj. دور revolved. Round; spherical. khat-i-mustadīr . 1. A curve line. 2. The circumference (of a circle).
(part. act. X. of دور Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Round, spherical. خط مستدير ḵẖat̤t̤-i-mustadīr, The circumference (of a circle).
subs: s.m. Circumference (of a circle); a curve.

ety: pass. part. of اصطفي 'to choose,' &c., viii of صفا (for صفو
def: part. adj. Chosen, elected; — s.m. 'The chosen one, the elect, or favourite' (of God), a name or title of Muḥammad; — a proper name.
more: (part. pass. VIII. of صفو Heb. ) adj. 1. Chosen, selected. 2. (subst.) One of the titles of Muḥammad.

ety: pass. part. of استفاد 'to gain or derive profit, advantage, or benefit' (from
def: part. Benefited; — received; acquired, gained; — understood; meant, intended.
more: (part. pass. X. of فيد) adj. Benefitted, received, acquired; understood.

ety: for A. مستعفٍ mustaʻfiṉ, act. part. of استعفي 'to seek to be exempted or excused (from
def: part. adj. Seeking to be exempted, or excused, or relieved (from); resigning (a charge or office); resigned; — s.m. One who has resigned (an office).
more: adj. عفو begging to be released from an obligation. Resigned (an office).
wety: From Arabic مُسْتَعْفًى (mustaʕfan).

ety: act. part. of استفاد; see mustafād
def: part. adj. & s.m. Anxious to profit (in any science, or study, &c.); intent on the acquisition (of wealth, or knowledge, &c.); imparting advantage or benefit; advantageous, benefiting; — gaining, profiting, acquiring; — a student; — one who imparts advantage; — a gainer
more: (part. act. X. of فيد) adj. Gaining, profiting, acquiring; ( met. ) A disciple.
subs: (-se), To derive benefit or advantage (from), to profit (by).

ety: act. part. of استفاض, x of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Spread abroad, diffused; divulged; — wide, ample; — favoured, obliged.
more: (part. act. X. of فيض Heb. ) adj. 1. Diffused, divulged. 2. Favoured, obliged.

ety: act. part. of استفسر 'to ask (one
def: part. adj. Asking for explanation or interpretation; questioning; inquisitive; — in need of explanation, &c.
more: (part. act. X. of فسر Heb. and Ch. ) adj. Asking for explanation or interpretation, questioning, inquisitive.

ety: pass. part. of استغاث 'to seek or desire aid or succour from,' x of غاث (for غوث
def: part. adj. Called upon for aid or succour or redress; — convoked
more: (part. pass. X. of غوث) Applied to for assistance or redress.

ety: act. part. of استغفر 'to beg forgiveness' (of God
def: part. adj. Asking forgiveness or pardon; penitent, repentant.
more: (part. act. X. of غفر) adj. Asking pardon, penitent, repentant.

ety: act. part. of استغاث; see mustag̠ās̤
def: s.m. A complainant, plaintiff, prosecutor.
more: n. m. غوث calling for aid. A complainant; prosecutor ( dāvedār ).
(part. act. X. of غوث) s. m. A complainant, plaintiff.

ety: for A. مستغنٍ mustag̠niṉ, act. part. of استغني, x of (and syn. with
def: adj. Free from want; in a state of competence; rich, wealthy; independent; able to dispense (with), or to do without; — content, satisfied; — indifferent (to), disdainful (of); boastful, proud, lofty, haughty, supercilious.
more: adj. غني was free. 1. Independent; free; indifferent ( be-parvā ). 2. Rich; wealthy. 3. Satisfied; contented. mustagnī-mizāj . Independent (disposition).
(part. act. X. of غني) adj. Rich, independent, satisfied, contented, purseproud.

ety: mustag̠nī, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: adv. Richly, independently, &c.

ety: part. pass. or act. of استغرق 'to take in the gross, to engross,' &c., x of غرق 'to sink, to be immersed'
def: part. adj. Immersed, plunged (into); drowned; — engrossed (in), absorbed, engaged, occupied; — hypothecated (as property).
more: adj. غرق 1. Drowned; immersed. 2. Absorbed; occupied. 3. Hypothecated.
(part. act. or pass. X. of غرق) adj. Immersed, drowned.

ety: pass. part. of استحبّ 'to love, like, approve,' x of حبّ 'to be or become loved or beloved'
def: part. Loved, liked, approved; — desirable
more: (part. pass. X. of حبّ Heb. ) adj. Desirable.
subs: (act. part. of the same verb), part. adj. Loving, affectionate, who holds dear.

ety: pass. & act. part. of استحق 'to demand as a right or due; to have a just title or claim to,' x of حقّ 'to be just, or right,' &c.
def: part. adj. Entitled (to), having a right or claim (to, -kā); worthy, deserving (of); meritorious; — rightful; — s.m. One who has a right or claim, a just claimant; — a deserving, or a meritorious person
more: adj. حق was right. 1. Entitled; having a claim. 2. Deserving; meritorious. mustahaq k. , v. a. To entitle; give a claim to. mustahaq h. , v. n. 1. To be entitled to. 2. To deserve.
(part. act. X. of حقّ Heb. ) adj. Worthy, having a right to, rightful.
subs: v.t. To make (one) entitled (to), to entitle, give (one) a claim (to, -kā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُسْتَحَقّ (mustahaqq), from Arabic مُسْتَحَقّ (mustaḥaqq).

ety: act. part. of استحال, x of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Altered, changed, transmuted; — absurd, impossible, &c. ( = muḥāl, q.v.).
more: (part. act. X. of حول) adj. Transmuted, changed.

ety: pass. part. of استحکم 'to be or become firm, or stable,' &c., x of حکم 'to restrain,' &c.; but quasi-pass. of احکم 'to make firm,' &c.
def: part. adj. Firm, stable, strong, solid; firmly established, fixed, settled; valid
more: (part. act. or pass. X. of حکم Heb. ) adj. Firm, established, strong, fast.
subs: v.t. To make or render firm, or strong, &c.; to strengthen; — to ratify, to confirm.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مُسْتَحْکَم (mustahkam), from Arabic مُسْتَحْكَم (mustaḥkam).

def: adj. حکم he gave judgment. 1. Strong; firm. 2. Established. 3. Valid. mustahkam k. , v. a. 1. To strengthen; make firm. 2. To ratify; confirm.

ety: act. part. of استہلك, x of (and syn. with
def: part. adj. Seeking destruction; leading to death; — ruinous, destructive; wasteful, spendthrift
more: (part. act. X. of هلک) adj. Seeking destruction, leading to death. ¶ mustahlak, (part. pass.) Destroyed, ruined.
subs: (pass. part. of the same verb), part. Ruined, destroyed; wasted, consumed, &c.

ety: pass. part. of استحسن 'to reckon or esteem good, comely, or pleasing, &c.; to approve,' &c., x of حسن 'to become good, &c.'
def: part. Esteemed good or comely, &c., approved; laudable, commendable; virtuous; beautiful, elegant
more: (part. pass. X. of حسن Heb. ) adj. Virtuous, approved, laudable, commendable, beautiful.

ety: pass. part. of استحضر 'to desire or demand the presence of,' x of حضر 'to be or become present'
def: part. Whose presence is desired or required; summoned to be present; — present; ready; called to mind; acquired.
more: (part. pass. X. of حضر) adj. Present.

ety: act. part. of استعدّ 'to make oneself ready, to prepare' (for
def: adj. Ready, prepared; prompt; on the alert, on the qui vive; on the point (of); — prepared, arranged, put in order (syn. āmāda); — able, capable, apt, fit, proper, worthy
more: adj. 1. Ready (آماده). 2. Capable; able. mustaid rahnā , v. a. To be ready; to await. mustaid k. , v. a. See āmādah k. mustaid h. , See āmādah h. sudūr-i-hukm ke mustaid rahnā . To await orders. mustaid'ī , n. f. Readiness; promptitude.
(part. act. X. of عدّ Heb. ) adj. Prepared, ready, capable, able, fit, proper, worthy, adept. مستعد علم mustaʼiﻌdd-i-iﻌlm, Learned.
subs: or mustaʻid honā (-meṅ, or -par), To remain, or to be, ready (in, or for, or against); to wait (for), to await
adj. Ready with knowledge or learning, learned
v.t. To make ready or prepared, to prepare; to put on the alert, &c.
